steps.title: Connect silence and influence
steps.content: Tom Towers enjoys a hidden kind of glory. He sits silently while politicians talk and thinks about how they are all in his power through his writing for The Jupiter.

steps.title: Reject unsupported motives
steps.content: Choice A adds public praise and inflated self-importance, which the passage does not establish. Choice C turns the provincial readers into his main aim. Choice D claims readers prefer him to politicians, another unsupported comparison. The text shows private satisfaction in influence. The answer is B.
